trimmedChitChat.startTrimmed: 00:00:00-00:05:44 — tech/screen-share troubleshooting, greetings, small talk about an upcoming conference (Seattle), Brian joining the room
trimmedChitChat.midTrimmed: 01:11:07-01:20:17 — spiritual motherhood tangent + medical-system critique with problematic sub-content (naturopath/PA categorical attack, sexual revolution analogy, Cochrane attack). NOT included in any lesson.
trimmedChitChat.endTrimmed: 01:30:17-end — personal goodbyes, chit-chat about Florida/Miami, Orlando conference, kids

lessons.title: The Menstrual Cycle: Brain, Ovary, and Uterus
lessons.topicSummary: Dr. Garcia introduces herself and her functional/bioidentical approach, then walks through the HPO axis: GnRH pulsatility, pituitary FSH/LH, follicular development, the estradiol-triggered LH surge, corpus luteum and progesterone, the endometrial garden analogy, and how synthetic birth control disrupts the natural rhythm. Closes with progesterone deficiency, PMS, anovulation, and recurrent miscarriage.

lessons.title: The Prolactin and Lactation Axis
lessons.topicSummary: Anatomy and function of the prolactin pathway: PIH, prolactin, oxytocin, milk production. Clinical signs of hyperprolactinemia (intermenstrual bleeding, galactorrhea), causes (SSRIs, pituitary adenoma, breast stimulation), and how elevated prolactin disrupts FSH/LH and prevents ovulation.

lessons.title: The Thyroid Axis and T4-to-T3 Conversion
lessons.topicSummary: TRH/TSH/T4/T3 cascade and why functional medicine treats 'thyroid dysfunction'. Covers Hashimoto's diagnosis via TPO antibodies and post-menopausal hypothyroidism prevalence.

lessons.title: Insulin, the Gut Microbiome, and PCOS
lessons.topicSummary: Insulin overreaction as a growth factor driving fat storage, fatty liver, and cardiovascular risk. PCOS pathophysiology. The gut: poor chewing, dysbiosis, leaky barrier, autoimmunity, and nutrient malabsorption.

lessons.title: Menopause and Bioidentical Hormone Replacement
lessons.topicSummary: Menopause physiology and the rising role of testosterone post-cyclicity. Standard of care vs functional approach adding T3 thyroid and physiologic testosterone for cardiovascular, bone, muscle, libido, and GU benefits. Q&A on why HRT demand is exploding, individual decision-making, and societal stress on women's hormones. ENDS at 01:11:07 (4267s) before the spiritual motherhood / medical system tangents.

lessons.title: Case Study: Hypothalamic Amenorrhea with PCOS Pattern
lessons.topicSummary: Live consultation: BMI 20, anti-inflammatory diet, low-dose naltrexone, low FSH, AMH 6.4 — hypothalamic-amenorrhea overlaid on PCOS pattern. Recommendations: increase naltrexone, IM progesterone reboot, red-light therapy, and nervous-system regulation.

notes: APPROVED 2026-05-01. 6 lessons. Note: there is a deliberate gap between lesson 5 end (4267s = 01:11:07) and lesson 6 start (4817s = 01:20:17). The 9:10 gap covers the trimmed spiritual motherhood + medical system critique content that is NOT included in either clip.
